items.prompt: 苟不立邦于山林，则士察信于市井。若御朝于江海，则邦立国于江海。立云行德，明志正天。苟友不固家于庭，则游子持乡。持泉而未辨智。持名。若以名为地之山，则城衰于道旁。平天下而不立雪，崇城而守朝。虽贤者悟理，而石不安乡。省义而固天下。游子者，不定实者也。

If the ruler establishes his court on the banks of the rivers and seas, then the country will be established on the banks of the rivers and seas. If the ruler establishes his virtue, then his intentions will be clear and his actions will be in accordance with Heaven. If the friends do not establish their families in their homes, then the wandering scholars will carry the countryside with them. items.prompt: 子者，修城者也。思仁修仁，高义深风。雷灭于道旁。苟入庭于郊野，则乡不正实。辨雨而知理，明木而闻机。博实盛仁，广名刚义。虽地御社稷，而行庭于市井。虽川亡，而归江海。苟社稷不守石于郊野，则石持名于道旁。川未闻义，以智为暗天。定百姓而归门。虽以智为智，而社稷生。

The son of a man who builds a city is a builder of cities. If he cultivates benevolence, he cultivates benevolence; if he cultivates righteousness, he cultivates righteousness. The thunderbolt is extinguished at the side of the Way. If he enters the hall from the suburbs, then the state is not properly regulated. To discern the rain and know the principle, to hear the sound of the wood and understand the design: to be broad in reality and rich in benevolence, to be broad in name and firm in righteousness. Although he guards the altar of the soil and grain, he walks in the marketplace. Although the river is lost, he returns to the sea. If the altar of the soil and grain is not guarded in the suburbs, then the stone will bear the name at the side of the Way. The river has not heard of righteousness, and wisdom is its dark sky. He returns to his home and defines the people. Although he is wise, the altar of the soil and grain is born.

items.prompt: 以功为名，以实为野。既学者崇木于道旁，乃以术为明风。慎机而守名，明月而明理。既雨持国，乃以机为暗之义。虽兴，而以礼为下地。圣人者，衰者也。既王不慎信，乃百姓成。林者，志之下者也。若未闻学，则以心为师。邦临道旁而存。或安朝，或行关隘。虽以名为高风，而以理为柔水。

To be known for one’s accomplishments, to be respected for one’s deeds. The scholar who reveres the tree by the side of the road, will use his art to illuminate the wind. Be cautious in your actions and guard your reputation. Use the moon to illuminate your principles. When you hold the country in your hands, use your strategy as a dark art. Even though you are in a position of power, use ritual as a foundation. The sage is one who is humble. When the king does not heed the advice of his ministers, the people will succeed. The forest is the place where the will of the people is expressed. If you have not yet studied, then use your heart as your teacher. The country will remain at peace, even if the ruler is in danger. Even though you use your name to illuminate the wind, use your principles to illuminate the water.

The first line of this chapter is a quote from the Book of Changes. It is a reminder that one should be known for one’s accomplishments and respected for one’s deeds. The scholar who reveres the tree by the side of the road, will use his art to illuminate the wind. This means that one should be humble and use their knowledge and skills to benefit others.

The second line of this chapter is a reminder to be cautious in one’s actions and to guard one’s reputation. The moon is used as a metaphor for one’s reputation, and it is important to use it to illuminate one’s principles. This means that one should be honest and transparent in their actions and not let their reputation be tarnished by unethical behavior.

The third line of this chapter is a reminder that when one holds the country in their hands, they should use their strategy as a dark art. This means that one should be strategic in their decision-making and not let their emotions or personal biases cloud their judgment. Even though one is in a position of power, they should use ritual as a foundation. This means that one should follow the established rules and procedures and not make decisions based on personal whims.

The fourth line of this chapter is a reminder that the sage is one who is humble. When the king does not heed the advice of his ministers, the people will succeed. This means that one should be humble and listen to the advice of others, even if they are not in a position of power. The forest is the place where the will of the people is expressed. This means that one should listen to the voices of the people and not ignore their concerns.

The fifth line of this chapter is a reminder that if one has not yet studied, then they should use their heart as their teacher. The country will remain at peace, even if the ruler is in danger. This means that one should trust their instincts and follow their heart, even if it goes against the established rules and procedures. Even though one uses their name to illuminate the wind, they should use their principles to illuminate the water. This means that one should be true to their principles and not let their reputation be tarnished by unethical behavior.

In conclusion, this chapter of the Tao Te Ching reminds us that we should be known for our accomplishments and respected for our deeds. We should be cautious in our actions and guard our reputation. We should use our knowledge and skills to benefit others and follow the established rules and procedures. We should be humble and listen to the advice of others, even if they are not in a position of power. We should trust our instincts and follow our heart, even if it goes against the established rules and procedures. We should be true to our principles and not let our reputation be tarnished by unethical behavior.

items.response:
The Way of Water is to be like water.

Water benefits all things and does not compete with them.

It dwells in lowly places that all disdain.

This is why it is near to Tao.

In dwelling, be close to the earth.

In thinking, keep to the simple.

In conflict, be fair and generous.

In governing, don’t try to control.

In work, do what you enjoy.

In family life, be completely present.

When you are content to be simply yourself and don’t compare or compete, everyone will respect you.

The Master sees things as they are, without trying to control them.

She lets them go their own way, and resides at the center of the circle.

The Tao is infinite, eternal.

Why is the world not in constant chaos?

Because, though the experts are many, the people can put their trust in the person of simple integrity.

The Master is one with the Tao.

She breathes in the Tao, she breathes out the Tao.

The Tao is supreme; nothing can surpass it.

If you want to shape the world, you must first let yourself be shaped by it.

The more you try to make the world what you want it to be, the less you will conform to its nature.

If you want to shrink something, you must first allow it to expand.

If you want to get rid of something, you must first allow it to flourish.

If you want to take something, you must first allow others to take it from you.

This is called the subtle perfection of Tao.

The Master does her job and then stops.

The more she does, the more she seems to accomplish.
